140	\section{Graded Hilbert polynomial}
141	\label{sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial}
142	
143	This section gives the graded-Hilbert-function construction that underlies
144	\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Working over a single fibre \(X_s\) with its
145	ample line bundle \(L_s\) and coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}_s\), it builds the
146	section graded ring (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and module
147	(\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), records their finite generation
148	(\cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}), and extracts the Hilbert polynomial
149	(\cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}) by combining the graded
150	Hilbert--Serre rationality of the Hilbert series
151	(\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) with Mathlib's polynomial-extraction
152	lemma (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}). The route uses only \(H^0\)
153	(global sections), so it is independent of higher coherent cohomology.
154	
155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
160	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
161	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
162	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
163	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
164	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
165	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
166	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
169	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
170	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
171	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
172	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
173	  %  the function
174	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
175	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
176	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
177	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
179	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
180	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
181	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
182	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
183	  \[
184	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
185	  \]
186	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
187	  the tensor product of sections
188	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
189	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
190	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
191	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
192	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
193	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
194	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
195	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
196	  polynomial ring).
197	\end{definition}
198	
199	\begin{definition}
200	[Section graded module]
201	  \label{def:sectionGradedModule}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule}
203	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing}
204	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
205	  % Polynomials" (read from
206	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
207	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
208	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
209	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
210	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
211	  %  the function
212	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
213	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
214	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
215	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
216	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
217	  I.7.}
218	  Let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on the fibre \(X_s\) and \(L_s\) a
219	  line bundle on \(X_s\). The \emph{section graded module} of \(\mathcal{F}_s\)
220	  twisted by \(L_s\) is the graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module
221	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing})
222	  \[
223	    M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)
224	    \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
225	  \]
226	  with degree-\(m\) component the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space
227	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) and scalar action given
228	  by the section tensor product
229	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
230	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m'})
231	  \to \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\). Its graded Hilbert
232	  function is \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)}
233	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
234	\end{definition}
235	
236	\begin{lemma}
237	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
238	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
239	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
240	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing}
241	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
242	  % Polynomials" (read from
243	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
244	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
245	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
246	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
247	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
248	  %  the function
249	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
250	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
251	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
252	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
253	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
254	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
255	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
256	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
257	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
258	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
259	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
260	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
261	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
262	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
263	\end{lemma}
264	
265	\begin{proof}
266	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
267	  Since \(L_s\) is ample and \(X_s\) is proper over \(\kappa(s)\), a Veronese
268	  power \(L_s^{\otimes e}\) is very ample and gives a closed immersion
269	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\). By Serre's theorem the section
270	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
271	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra, so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
272	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), the same ampleness yields, again by Serre, that
273	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
274	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
275	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
276	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
277	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
278	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
279	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
280	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
281	\end{proof}
282	
283	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
284	  graded series]
285	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
286	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
287	  \mathlibok
288	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
289	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
290	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
291	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
292	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
293	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
294	  equals \(h(n)\):
295	  \[
296	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
297	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
298	  \]
299	  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
300	  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
301	  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
302	  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
303	  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
304	\end{lemma}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}[Additivity of \(\dim_\kappa\) on a short exact sequence
307	  (rank--nullity)]
308	  \label{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib}
309	  \lean{Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank}
310	  \mathlibok
311	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
312	  (\texttt{Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity}).}
313	  Let \(\kappa\) be a field, \(V\) a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space and
314	  \(W \subseteq V\) a subspace. Then
315	  \[
316	    \dim_\kappa (V / W) + \dim_\kappa W \;=\; \dim_\kappa V .
317	  \]
318	  Equivalently, \(\dim_\kappa = \operatorname{finrank}_\kappa\) is additive on a
319	  short exact sequence \(0 \to W \to V \to V/W \to 0\) of finite-dimensional
320	  \(\kappa\)-vector spaces. This is the additivity that converts the degreewise
321	  short exact sequences of graded components into additive relations among the
322	  coefficients of the Hilbert series.
323	\end{lemma}
324	
325	\begin{lemma}[The rational power series \((1 - X)^{-d}\)]
326	  \label{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
327	  \lean{PowerSeries.invOneSubPow}
328	  \mathlibok
329	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
330	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown}).}
331	  Let \(S\) be a commutative ring and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then \((1 - X)^d\) is a
332	  unit in the power series ring \(S\llbracket X\rrbracket\), and Mathlib names its
333	  inverse
334	  \[
335	    \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(S, d) \;=\; (1 - X)^{-d}
336	      \;\in\; S\llbracket X\rrbracket^{\times}.
337	  \]
338	  Together with the coercion
339	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries} : F[X] \to F\llbracket X\rrbracket\) of a
340	  polynomial to its power series, this is the data in which a rational Hilbert
341	  series \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\) is expressed; it is the same rational-series shape
342	  whose eventual coefficients are extracted by
343	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
344	\end{lemma}
345	
346	\begin{lemma}\leanok
347	[Graded Hilbert--Serre: rationality of the Hilbert series]
348	  \label{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}
349	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational}
350	  \uses{lem:finrank_ses_additive_mathlib, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib,
351	    def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
352	    lem:ratHilb_bump, def:graded_subquotientHilb,
353	    lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}
354	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", \S "Noetherian graded
355	  % rings", Proposition \texttt{proposition-graded-hilbert-polynomial} (Tag
356	  % 00K1) (read from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13893-L13905).
357	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
358	  % "Suppose that $S$ is a Noetherian graded ring
359	  %  and $M$ a finite graded $S$-module. Consider the
360	  %  function
361	  %  $$
362	  %  \mathbf{Z} \longrightarrow K'_0(S_0), \quad
363	  %  n \longmapsto [M_n]
364	  %  $$
365	  %  see Lemma \ref{lemma-graded-module-fg}.
366	  %  If $S_{+}$ is generated by elements of degree $1$,
367	  %  then this function is a numerical polynomial."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Commutative Algebra", Tag 00K1
369	  (proposition on graded Hilbert polynomials); cf.\ [Nitsure], \S 1 (Snapper's
370	  Lemma), [Hartshorne], I.7.}
371	  Let \(R\) be a Noetherian graded ring whose degree-zero part is a field
372	  \(\kappa\) and which is generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra by finitely many
373	  elements, and let \(M\) be a finitely generated graded \(R\)-module with
374	  finite-dimensional graded components \(M_n\). Then there exist
375	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
376	  such that for all \(n > N\)
377	  \[
378	    \dim_{\kappa} M_n \;=\; \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr);
379	  \]
380	  that is, the Hilbert series \(\sum_{n \ge 0} (\dim_\kappa M_n)\, X^n\) is, up to
381	  a polynomial correction in low degrees, the rational function
382	  \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\), expressed in the power-series form
383	  \(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p) \cdot \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\)
384	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
385	
386	  \emph{Status.} This is the substantive half of the graded Hilbert--Serre
387	  theorem (rationality of the Hilbert series of a finitely generated graded
388	  module). It is not covered by Mathlib: \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
389	  supplies only the converse polynomial-extraction step from a rational series, not
390	  the production of the rational series from the module. The argument is realised by
391	  an \emph{ambient subquotient induction} (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}):
392	  the whole induction ranges over pairs of homogeneous submodules \(N' \le N\) of the
393	  single fixed graded module \(M\), and the Hilbert function is the ambient difference
394	  \(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa(N \cap M_n) - \dim_\kappa(N' \cap M_n)\). The class of such
395	  pairs is closed under the kernel and cokernel of multiplication by a degree-one
396	  element (each presented again as an ambient subquotient pair), so the inductive step
397	  never forms a graded quotient ring \(R/(x)\), a graded quotient module \(M/xM\), or a
398	  regrading of a derived carrier. The genuine Mathlib gap is thereby \emph{avoided}
399	  rather than filled; the residual obligation is the short ambient bookkeeping of the
400	  subquotient blocks (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb},
401	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker},
402	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff},
403	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer},
404	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}), each of which manipulates only ambient
405	  families \(n \mapsto N_{\mathrm{aux}} \cap M_n\) of submodules of \(M\).
406	  % NOTE: The power-series rationality toolkit (\cref{subsec:isRatHilb}) is formalized
407	  %   and axiom-clean; the present theorem is a closed, axiom-clean Lean declaration
408	  %   (gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational). The ambient subquotient route
409	  %   (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb}) sidesteps missing graded-quotient API by
410	  %   working with ambient families of submodules of \(M\).
411	\end{lemma}
412	
413	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: [Stacks Project], Tag 00K1, proof, inductive step (read
414	% from references/hilbert-serre-algebra.tex, L13938-L13947).
415	% "Let $\overline{M} = M/xM$. Note that the map $x : M \to M$
416	%  is {\it not} a map of graded $S$-modules, since it does
417	%  not map $M_d$ into $M_d$. Namely, for each $d$ we have the
418	%  following short exact sequence
419	%  $$
420	%  0 \to M_d \xrightarrow{x} M_{d + 1} \to \overline{M}_{d + 1} \to 0
421	%  $$
422	%  This proves that $[M_{d + 1}] - [M_d] = [\overline{M}_{d + 1}]$.
423	%  Hence we win by Lemma \ref{lemma-numerical-polynomial}."
424	\begin{proof}
425	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero, lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq,
426	    lem:ratHilb_bump, lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff,
427	    def:graded_subquotientHilb, lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker,
428	    lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff,
429	    lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer, lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb,
430	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSupIndep,
431	    lem:graded_homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_eq, lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib,
432	    lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib,
433	    lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib}
434	    \leanok
435	  We argue with the abstract graded \(\kappa\)-algebra
436	  \(R = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} R_n\), whose degree-zero part \(R_0 = \kappa\) is a
437	  field, and the finitely generated graded \(R\)-module
438	  \(M = \bigoplus_{n \ge 0} M_n\) with finite-dimensional components \(M_n\);
439	  there is no appeal to any geometric realisation of \(R\) or \(M\).
440	
441	  \emph{Reduction to generation in degree \(1\).} Since \(R\) is Noetherian and
442	  finitely generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra, it is generated by finitely many
443	  homogeneous elements of positive degree. Replacing \(R\) by a Veronese
444	  subalgebra \(R^{(e)} = \bigoplus_n R_{en}\) for a suitable \(e\) and re-grading
445	  (and \(M\) by the corresponding Veronese module), we may assume \(R\) is
446	  generated by its degree-one part, \(\kappa[R_1] = R\). The degree-one part
447	  \(R_1\) is then a finite-dimensional \(\kappa\)-vector space whose \(\kappa\)-basis
448	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) generates \(R\) as a \(\kappa\)-algebra; \(r = \dim_\kappa R_1\)
449	  is the number on which we induct.
450	  % NOTE: The existence of finitely many degree-one generators (the number r the
451	  % induction is on) is expected to follow from the standing hypotheses --
452	  % "finitely generated κ-algebra" together with "generated in degree 1" -- so no
453	  % new hypothesis on gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational is anticipated. To be
454	  % confirmed when the prover formalizes the assembly: degree-1 generation gives
455	  % R₁ as a finite-dimensional κ-vector space spanning R as a κ-algebra, and a
456	  % κ-basis of R₁ is a finite degree-one generating set.
457	
458	  \emph{Set-up of the ambient data.} Regard \(M\) as the fixed ambient graded
459	  \(\kappa\)-module with internal decomposition \(n \mapsto \mathcal{M}_n := M_n\)
460	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); the components are finite-dimensional. For each
461	  basis vector \(x_i \in R_1\) let \(t_i : M \to M\) be multiplication by \(x_i\), a
462	  \(\kappa\)-linear endomorphism that raises degree by one (it sends \(M_n\) into
463	  \(M_{n+1}\), \cref{lem:isHomogeneousElem_graded_smul_mathlib}). Since \(R\) is
464	  commutative the \(t_i\) pairwise commute, and they trivially preserve the pair of
465	  homogeneous submodules \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\). Because \(R\) is generated by
466	  \(x_1, \dots, x_r\) and \(M\) is finitely generated over \(R\), \(M\) is finitely
467	  generated as a module over the polynomial ring \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_r]\) acting
468	  through the \(t_i\); this is the finiteness condition required below.
469	
470	  \emph{Invoking the ambient subquotient induction.} Apply
471	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} to the pair \((N, N') = (\top, \bot)\)
472	  with the \(r\) commuting degree-\((+1)\) endomorphisms \(t_1, \dots, t_r\). It
473	  yields \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) of order \(r\) for the ambient subquotient Hilbert
474	  function of this pair (\cref{def:graded_subquotientHilb}),
475	  \[
476	    h(n) \;=\; \dim_\kappa(\top \cap \mathcal{M}_n) - \dim_\kappa(\bot \cap \mathcal{M}_n)
477	      \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n - 0 \;=\; \dim_\kappa M_n .
478	  \]
479	  Hence \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto \dim_\kappa M_n,\, r)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}):
480	  there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\), \(d := r \in \mathbb{N}\) and \(N \in \mathbb{N}\)
481	  with
482	  \[
483	    \dim_\kappa M_n \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d}\bigr)
484	    \qquad (n > N),
485	  \]
486	  which is the claim, with pole order \(d = r\). The internal mechanics of
487	  \cref{lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb} -- the base case via
488	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}, and the inductive step assembling the
489	  kernel/cokernel closure (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker}), the degreewise
490	  difference identity (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_degreewise_diff}, built on
491	  \(D_5\), \cref{lem:graded_degreewise_finrank_diff}) and the finiteness transfer
492	  (\cref{lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer}) into
493	  \cref{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq} and \cref{lem:ratHilb_bump} -- are the Stacks~00K1
494	  induction realised entirely with ambient homogeneous submodules of \(M\).
495	\end{proof}
496	
497	\subsection{The rationality toolkit \texorpdfstring{\(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\)}{IsRatHilb}}
498	\label{subsec:isRatHilb}
499	% NOTE: Declarations in this subsection are currently \texttt{private} in the Lean
500	% source; \lean{} pins may not resolve until they are moved to a dedicated module.
501	
502	The proof of \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational} runs the Stacks~00K1 induction
503	on the number of degree-one generators. Each inductive step manipulates the
504	\emph{shape} of the Hilbert series -- a numerator polynomial over a power of
505	\((1-X)\) -- rather than the module itself. We isolate that bookkeeping in a
506	single predicate \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}\) on functions \(f : \mathbb{N} \to
507	\mathbb{Q}\), together with the closure lemmas the induction consumes. These are
508	project-internal helpers realising standard steps of the Stacks~00K1 argument at
509	the level of power series; Mathlib supplies only the converse extraction
510	(\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}).
511	
512	\begin{lemma}\leanok
513	[Coefficients of \((1-X)^{-1}\) times a series are partial sums]
514	  \label{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
515	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul}
516	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
517	  Let \(F \in \mathbb{Q}\llbracket X\rrbracket\) be a power series. Then for every
518	  \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) the \(n\)-th coefficient of
519	  \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, 1)\cdot F = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\)
520	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}) is the partial sum of the coefficients of
521	  \(F\):
522	  \[
523	    \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl((1-X)^{-1}\cdot F\bigr)
524	    \;=\; \sum_{k=0}^{n} \bigl[X^k\bigr] F .
525	  \]
526	  Equivalently, multiplication by \((1-X)^{-1} = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) is the
527	  summation operator on coefficient sequences. Proved directly in Lean from the
528	  identity \(\operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q},1) = \sum_{m\ge 0} X^m\) and
529	  the Cauchy product.
530	\end{lemma}
531	
532	\begin{lemma}\leanok
533	[Antidifference of a rational Hilbert series]
534	  \label{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
535	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rationalHilbert_antidiff}
536	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
537	  Let \(H, \delta : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\), let \(q \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and
538	  \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). Suppose that for all \(n > N\) the sequence \(\delta\)
539	  is the coefficient sequence of the rational series \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\),
540	  \[
541	    \delta(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\bigr),
542	  \]
543	  and that \(\delta\) is the first difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\), i.e.
544	  \(H(n+1) - H(n) = \delta(n+1)\) for all \(n > N\). Then there is a numerator
545	  polynomial \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) such that for all \(n > N\)
546	  \[
547	    H(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr).
548	  \]
549	  That is, summing a coefficient sequence (anti-differencing) raises the pole
550	  order by one: the partial-sum series of \(q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\) equals
551	  \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\) up to a polynomial correction in low degrees. This is
552	  the power-series heart of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step.
553	\end{lemma}
554	
555	\begin{proof}
556	  \uses{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
557	  \leanok
558	  Write \(F = q\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\). Multiplying by
559	  \((1-X)^{-1}\) and using the factorisation
560	  \((1-X)^{-(e+1)} = (1-X)^{-1}\cdot(1-X)^{-e}\)
561	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}), \cref{lem:coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul} gives
562	  \(\bigl[X^m\bigr]\bigl(q\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr) = \sum_{k=0}^{m}[X^k]F\), the
563	  partial sums of \(F\). On the other hand, telescoping the first-difference
564	  hypothesis from \(N+1\) expresses \(H(N+1+j)\) as \(H(N+1)\) plus a window sum
565	  of the coefficients of \(F\). Splitting the partial sum
566	  \(\sum_{k=0}^{n}[X^k]F\) at \(N+2\) and absorbing the resulting constant
567	  discrepancy into a numerator \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\) (a constant function is the
568	  order-\((e+1)\) coefficient sequence of \(C\,(1-X)^{e}\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)} =
569	  C\,(1-X)^{-1}\)), the explicit numerator
570	  \(p = C\,(1-X)^{e} + q\) realises \(H(n)\) as
571	  \([X^n]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)}\bigr)\) for all \(n > N\).
572	\end{proof}
573	
574	\begin{definition}\leanok
575	[Rational Hilbert function]
576	  \label{def:ratHilb}
577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb}
578	  \uses{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
579	  \textit{Source: Stacks 00K1.}
580	  For a function \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), say
581	  that \(f\) is a \emph{rational Hilbert function of order \(d\)}, written
582	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\), when there exist a numerator polynomial
583	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and an \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) such that for all \(n > N\)
584	  \[
585	    f(n) \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\bigr)
586	    \;=\; \bigl[X^n\bigr]\bigl(\operatorname{toPowerSeries}(p)\cdot
587	      \operatorname{invOneSubPow}(\mathbb{Q}, d)\bigr)
588	  \]
589	  (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}). This is the ``eventually equal to the
590	  coefficient sequence of a rational series \(p\cdot(1-X)^{-d}\)'' predicate that
591	  the graded Hilbert--Serre induction tracks; its conclusion shape is exactly the
592	  one consumed by \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}.
593	\end{definition}
594	
595	\begin{lemma}\leanok
596	[Base case: eventually-zero functions are rational of order \(0\)]
597	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofEventuallyZero}
598	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero}
599	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
600	  If \(f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) is eventually zero -- there is
601	  \(N\) with \(f(n) = 0\) for all \(n > N\) -- then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, 0)\)
602	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}). Indeed \(f\) is then the coefficient sequence of the
603	  numerator polynomial \(0\) over \((1-X)^{0} = 1\). This is the base case of the
604	  Stacks~00K1 induction (no degree-one generators). Proved directly in Lean.
605	\end{lemma}
606	
607	\begin{lemma}\leanok
608	[Raising the pole order]
609	  \label{lem:ratHilb_bump}
610	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.bump}
611	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}
612	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
613	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d+1)\): multiplying the numerator by \((1-X)\) cancels
614	  one factor of \((1-X)^{-1}\), so the same coefficient sequence is realised at
615	  pole order \(d+1\). This lets two rational Hilbert functions of different
616	  orders be brought to a common denominator. Proved directly in Lean using
617	  \((1-X)\cdot(1-X)^{-(d+1)} = (1-X)^{-d}\) (\cref{lem:invOneSubPow_mathlib}).
618	\end{lemma}
619	
620	\begin{lemma}\leanok
621	[Closure under difference]
622	  \label{lem:ratHilb_sub}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.sub}
624	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
625	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, d)\)
626	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}) at the same order \(d\), then
627	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n) - g(n),\, d)\): the numerator of the
628	  difference is the difference of the numerators (over the common denominator
629	  \((1-X)^{-d}\)). Proved directly in Lean.
630	\end{lemma}
631	
632	\begin{lemma}\leanok
633	[Closure under right shift]
634	  \label{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.shiftRight}
636	  \uses{def:ratHilb}
637	  If \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(f, d)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) then
638	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(n \mapsto f(n-1),\, d)\): the right shift on coefficient
639	  sequences is multiplication of the series by \(X\), so the numerator
640	  \(p\) is replaced by \(X\cdot p\) at the same pole order. Proved directly in
641	  Lean.
642	\end{lemma}
643	
644	\begin{lemma}\leanok
645	[Antidifference step for the predicate]
646	  \label{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}
647	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.antidiff}
648	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
649	  Let \(H, g : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(e, N \in \mathbb{N}\). If
650	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}) and \(g\) is the first
651	  difference of \(H\) beyond \(N\) (\(H(n+1) - H(n) = g(n+1)\) for all
652	  \(n > N\)), then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\). This is the predicate-level
653	  packaging of \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}: a finite difference raises
654	  the pole order by one, the inductive heart of graded Hilbert--Serre.
655	\end{lemma}
656	
657	\begin{proof}
658	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff}
659	  \leanok
660	  Unfolding \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(g, e)\) supplies a numerator \(q\) and a
661	  threshold \(N_g\) with \(g(n) = [X^n](q\cdot(1-X)^{-e})\) for \(n > N_g\).
662	  Applying \cref{lem:rationalHilbert_antidiff} with this \(q\) and threshold
663	  \(\max(N, N_g)\) -- whose two hypotheses are exactly the rational form of \(g\)
664	  and the first-difference identity for \(H\) -- yields a numerator \(p\) with
665	  \(H(n) = [X^n](p\cdot(1-X)^{-(e+1)})\) for \(n > \max(N, N_g)\), i.e.
666	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(H, e+1)\).
667	\end{proof}
668	
669	\begin{lemma}\leanok
670	[Inductive-step engine from a degreewise short exact sequence]
671	  \label{lem:ratHilb_ofDiffEq}
672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq}
673	  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
674	  Let \(h_M, h_C, h_K : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{Q}\) and \(d, N \in \mathbb{N}\).
675	  Suppose \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_C, d)\) and \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_K, d)\)
676	  (\cref{def:ratHilb}), and that for all \(n > N\)
677	  \[
678	    h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) \;=\; h_C(n+1) - h_K(n).
679	  \]
680	  Then \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\). This packages the entire power-series
681	  side of the Stacks~00K1 inductive step: \(h_M\) is the Hilbert function of
682	  \(M\), and \(h_C, h_K\) are the Hilbert functions of the cokernel
683	  \(C = M/xM\) and kernel \(K = \ker(x : M \to M(1))\) of multiplication by a
684	  degree-one element \(x\); the displayed identity is the alternating-sum
685	  consequence of the degreewise short exact sequence
686	  \(0 \to K_n \to M_n \xrightarrow{x} M_{n+1} \to C_{n+1} \to 0\). Given the
687	  inductive hypothesis that \(h_C, h_K\) are rational of order \(d\), the
688	  conclusion is rationality of \(h_M\) at order \(d+1\).
689	\end{lemma}
690	
691	\begin{proof}
692	  \uses{lem:ratHilb_antidiff, lem:ratHilb_sub, lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}
693	  \leanok
694	  Set \(g(n) = h_C(n) - h_K(n-1)\). By closure under right shift
695	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_shiftRight}) \(n \mapsto h_K(n-1)\) is rational of order
696	  \(d\), and by closure under difference (\cref{lem:ratHilb_sub}) so is \(g\). The
697	  hypothesis rewrites as \(h_M(n+1) - h_M(n) = g(n+1)\) for \(n > N\) (since
698	  \(g(n+1) = h_C(n+1) - h_K(n)\)). Applying the antidifference step
699	  (\cref{lem:ratHilb_antidiff}) to \(H = h_M\) and this \(g\) gives
700	  \(\mathrm{IsRatHilb}(h_M, d+1)\).
701	\end{proof}
702	
703	\begin{theorem}[Hilbert polynomial from the section module]
704	  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
705	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomialOfSectionModule}
706	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
707	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
708	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
709	  % Polynomials" (read from
710	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L478).
711	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
712	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
713	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
714	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
715	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
716	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
717	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
718	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
719	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$."
720	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
721	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
722	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
723	  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
724	  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
725	  \[
726	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
727	    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
728	  \]
729	  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
730	  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.
731	\end{theorem}
732	
733	\begin{proof}
734	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
735	    lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
736	  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
737	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
738	  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and
739	  degree-zero part the field \(\kappa(s)\). Applying the abstract graded
740	  Hilbert--Serre rationality lemma (\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) to the
741	  graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra \(R = R(X_s, L_s)\) and the finitely generated
742	  graded module \(M = M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) exhibits the graded Hilbert
743	  function
744	  \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
745	  as the eventual coefficient function of a rational series: there are
746	  \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
747	  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
748	  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). Apply
749	  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} over the characteristic-zero field
750	  \(F = \mathbb{Q}\) to this pair \((p, d)\): it yields a unique polynomial
751	  \(h \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) whose values \(h(n)\) eventually equal
752	  \([X^n]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\). Setting
753	  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} := h\) (with the variable renamed \(\lambda\)) gives a
754	  polynomial with \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = \dim_{\kappa(s)}
755	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) for \(m \gg 0\). Uniqueness
756	  is the \(\exists!\) clause of \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}: any two
757	  polynomials agreeing with the Hilbert function for \(m \gg 0\) agree at
758	  infinitely many points, hence are equal.

2991	\begin{definition}[The over-site/open-subspace sheaf equivalence]
2992	  \label{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}
2993	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.overEquivalence_sheafCongr}
2994	  \uses{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib, lem:equivalence_sheafCongr_mathlib,
2995	    lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite}
2996	  \textit{Keystone of the topological layer of bridge C.}
2997	  For any category \(A\), the site equivalence \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\)
2998	  (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}) lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
2999	  \[

# Mathlib Analogist Report

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
quot-transport

## Iteration
030

## Question
To finish gap1 (`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` for `M : (Spec R).Modules`, `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`), transport
each slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` (for `D(r) ≤ q.X i`) to
`IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` on `Spec R_r`. (1) Does Mathlib already provide gap1 or a
substantial fraction — a restriction/pullback functor on `(Spec R).Modules`, a presentation
pullback/restriction lemma? (2) Is the `q.X i` sieve-`over` route canonical, or is there a cleaner
slice-free path? (3) Is there a Mathlib idiom for restriction/pullback of `SheafOfModules` along a
scheme morphism, and `Presentation.pullback`/`.map`? (4) PROCEED or ALIGN-WITH-MATHLIB?

## Verdicts (summary)

| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| A. Restriction-to-open functor on `(Spec R).Modules` exists (obstacle #1 claim) | PROCEED (claim is wrong) | informational |
| B. `over`-slice / `q.presentation i` route for the transport sub-step | PROCEED (canonical; timeout tameable) | informational |
| C. `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge (`overRestrictIso`) | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL | major |
| D. Section-localization descent (the real keystone) | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL | major |

## Direct answers to the four questions

**Q1 — does Mathlib provide gap1 / a fraction of it?**
- gap1 itself: **NO**. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M`
  (`Modules/Tilde.lean:340`); only `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (needs a **global**
  `M.Presentation`, `:398`) and the easy `presentationTilde`/`(tilde M).IsQuasicoherent` (`:373,394`).
  Source comment `Tilde.lean:310` states QCoh = essImage is deferred. Confirms `quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md`.
- Restriction/pullback functor: **YES** (this is the substantial fraction). `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
  (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:167`) and a full open-immersion `Restriction` section: `restrictFunctor`
  (`:319`), `restrict`/`restrict_obj` (`:325,328`), `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (`:371`),
  `restrictFunctorComp` (`:392`), `restrictStalkNatIso` (`:424`). The project already uses
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι` in `IsLocallyFreeOfRank` (`QuotScheme.lean:255`).
- Presentation transport: **YES** — `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map (F) (η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S)`
  for colimit-preserving `F` (`Quasicoherent.lean:179`), plus `Presentation.ofIsIso` (`:132`).
  `pullback`/`restrictFunctor` are left adjoints sending `unit ↦ unit`, so `.map` applies.

**Q2 — is the sieve-`over` route canonical, or is there a slice-free path?**
- There is **no slice-free path** (Q2(i): NO). `IsQuasicoherent` is *defined* only via
  `QuasicoherentData` (`Quasicoherent.lean:201,249`), whose presentations live on the abstract slice
  `M.over (X i) = (pushforward (𝟙_)).obj M` (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:53`). Pulling a local
  presentation out of `[M.IsQuasicoherent]` **must** touch the slice.
- The slice route **is** the canonical idiom for that sub-step: `QuasicoherentData.bind`
  (`Quasicoherent.lean:360`) is the worked template — `Presentation.map e.inverse` with
  `e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv g)`
  (`PushforwardContinuous.lean:305`, `Sites/Over.lean:469–489`).
- The **timeout is not structural**: `bind` defeats the same `(sheafToPresheaf (J.over …)).IsRightAdjoint`
  / `HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up with `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`
  (+ an `#adaptation_note`). The prover hit it because that option was not in force.
- Q2(ii): the project's engine **already** reduces `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` to per-basic-open
  `IsLocalizedModule` (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`, `QuotScheme.lean:653`).
  That reduction is done; the residue is producing the per-basic-open localization, which is the
  descent (Decision D), not more naturality bookkeeping.

**Q3 — restriction/pullback of `SheafOfModules` along a scheme morphism + `Presentation.map`?**
- **YES** to all: `Scheme.Modules.pullback`/`restrictFunctor` (Q1), `SheafOfModules.pullback`
  (`Sheaf.lean:167–168` wraps it), and `Presentation.map` (Q1). Build on these, not a hand-rolled
  `over`-slice transport.

**Q4 — verdict: PROCEED, with a corrected decomposition.** The route is not the wrong shape, but the
plan mislocates the work. Build the two genuinely-missing lemmas (C, D); the rest is Mathlib glue.

## Major

- **C. The `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge is the load-bearing missing ingredient (`NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL`).**
  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` needs `M : (Spec S).Modules` and a *geometric* presentation, but
  `q.presentation i` lives on the abstract slice `SheafOfModules ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (q.X i))`.
  Mathlib has the constituents — `Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor` (`OpenImmersion.lean:87`), the
  basicOpen↔`Spec R_r` isos (`AffineScheme.lean:566–572`, `isLocalization_basicOpen`), the iterated
  slice equivs (`Sites/Over.lean`) — but **no** equivalence `J.over U ≃ Opens(U.toScheme)` carrying
  `R.over U ≅ U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf`, hence no `M.over U ≅ M.restrict U.ι`. Build `overRestrictIso`
  as the single lemma that touches the slice site (use `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`).

- **D. The section-localization descent is the keystone, not the transport (`NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL`).**
  Even granting `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` per `Spec R_r`, assembling the global-over-`R`
  statement the engine consumes (`∀ f, IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (Γ(M,⊤)→Γ(M,D f))`) needs the
  sheaf-equalizer + flat-localization descent (Hartshorne II.5.3 / Stacks 01HA): `M.isSheaf`,
  finite cover, `IsLocalization.flat`. `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation`
  (`QuotScheme.lean:686`) does NOT close it — that needs a *global* presentation of `M` on `Spec R`,
  not per-`D(r)` data. Same irreducible content flagged in `quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md`.

## Informational

- A/B (PROCEED). Obstacle #1 ("no restriction functor on `(Spec R).Modules`") is **factually wrong**:
  `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`/`pullback` live in `Modules/Sheaf.lean` (a Restriction section the
  prover apparently excluded from `{Presheaf, Sheaf, Tilde}`). The slice route is canonical for the
  transport sub-step and its timeout is tameable. Do not hand-roll a restriction functor.
- Recommended build order (detail in the persistent file): (1) `overRestrictIso` [C];
  (2) per-affine local-tilde via `Presentation.map` (iterated slice → `overRestrictIso` →
  `pullback` of `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r`) + `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` [P1]; (3) section-localization
  descent [D]; (4) feed the existing `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`.
- The standing memory `quot-quasicoherentdata-slice-transport-wall` is now partly stale: the wall is
  not "no restriction functor" but "no `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge + descent is the keystone", and the
  slice-statement timeout is dissolved by `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`.

## Persistent file
- `analogies/quot-gap1-transport.md` — design-rationale + four-step decomposition captured for future iters.

Overall verdict: PROCEED on a corrected decomposition — the `over`-slice route is the canonical (and
tameable) idiom for the presentation-transport sub-step and a restriction functor already exists, but
gap1's real work is the two new lemmas `overRestrictIso` (the `over U ↔ Spec R_r` bridge) and the
section-localization descent.

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Shapes.BinaryProducts

/-!
# Pushforward of sheaves of modules

Assume that categories `C` and `D` are equipped with Grothendieck topologies, and
that `F : C ⥤ D` is a continuous functor.
Then, if `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R` is
a morphism of sheaves of rings, we construct the pushforward functor
`pushforward φ : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w v' u' v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u

open CategoryTheory Functor

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {D' : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} D'] {D'' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} D'']
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules that is induced by a continuous functor `F`
and a morphism of sheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J K).obj R`. -/
@[simps map_val, simps -isSimp obj_val]
noncomputable def pushforward : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S where
  obj M :=
    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).obj M.val
      isSheaf := ((F.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ J K).obj ⟨_, M.isSheaf⟩).property }
  map f :=
    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).map f.val }

/-- Given `M : SheafOfModules R` and `X : D`, this is the restriction of `M`
over the sheaf of rings `R.over X` on the category `Over X`. -/
noncomputable abbrev over (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) (X : D) : SheafOfModules.{v} (R.over X) :=
  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).obj M

/-- Given a map `f : M ⟶ N` between sheaves of modules over `R`, this is the restriction
to the map `M.over X ⟶ N.over X` between sheaves of modules over `R.over X`. -/
noncomputable abbrev Hom.over {M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (X : D) :
    M.over X ⟶ N.over X :=
  (pushforward.{v} (𝟙 _)).map f

section

variable (R) in
/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
the identify functor of the category of sheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardId :
    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  Iso.refl _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms of sheaves of rings are isomorphic. -/
noncomputable
def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
    pushforward.{v} φ ≅ pushforward.{v} ψ :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ (SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _).preimageIso
    (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
      fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_symm
    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) :
  (pushforwardCongr e).symm = pushforwardCongr e.symm := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_val_app
    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
  ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_val_app
    {φ ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R} (e : φ = ψ) (M U x) :
  ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl

section

variable {K' : GrothendieckTopology D'} {K'' : GrothendieckTopology D''}
  {G : D ⥤ D'} {R' : Sheaf K' RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous G K K'] [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G) J K']
  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').obj R')

#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of sheaves of modules
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) :=
  Iso.refl _

-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_val_app (M U x) :
  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl

-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app (M U x) :
  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl

  (ψ' : R' ⟶ (G'.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K' K'').obj R'')

end Adjunction

section Equivalence

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]
  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
  (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
  (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =
    ψ.hom ≫ eqv.inverse.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ eqv.functor.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)

/-- If `e : C ≌ D`, then the pushforwards along `e.functor` and `e.inverse` forms an equivalence. -/
noncomputable
def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S where
  functor := pushforward.{v} φ
  inverse := pushforward.{v} ψ
  unitIso :=
    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.inverse eqv.functor K J K
    (pushforwardId _).symm ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.counitIso ≪≫
      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).symm
  counitIso :=
    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.functor eqv.inverse J K J
    pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.unitIso ≪≫
      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
  functor_unitIso_comp :=
    (pushforwardPushforwardAdj eqv.toAdjunction φ ψ H₁ H₂).left_triangle_components

-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
      M.val.map (eqv.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl

-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
      M.val.map (eqv.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl

end Equivalence

universe u

open CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable (X Y : Scheme.{u})

/-- The underlying sheaf of rings of a scheme. -/
abbrev ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat.{u} X :=
  (sheafCompose _ (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat.{u})).obj X.sheaf

/-- The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
nonrec abbrev PresheafOfModules := PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj

variable {X Y} in
/-- The morphism of sheaves of rings corresponding to a morphism of schemes. -/
def Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).sheafPushforwardContinuous
      _ _ _).obj X.ringCatSheaf where
  hom := Functor.whiskerRight f.c _

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

end GrothendieckTopology

variable {J}

/-- Given `F : Sheaf J A` and `X : C`, this is the pullback of `F` on `J.over X`. -/
abbrev Sheaf.over {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] (F : Sheaf J A) (X : C) :
    Sheaf (J.over X) A := (J.overPullback A X).obj F

section

-- TODO: Generalize this section to arbitrary precoverages.

variable (K : Precoverage C) [K.HasPullbacks] [K.IsStableUnderBaseChange]

/-- The Grothendieck topology on `Over X`, obtained from localizing the topology generated
by the precoverage `K`, is generated by the preimage of `K`. -/
lemma over_toGrothendieck_eq_toGrothendieck_comap_forget (X : C) :
    K.toGrothendieck.over X = (K.comap (Over.forget X)).toGrothendieck := by
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  · intro ⟨Y, right, (s : Y ⟶ X)⟩ R hR
    obtain ⟨(R : Sieve Y), rfl⟩ := (Sieve.overEquiv _).symm.surjective R
    simp +instances only [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff, Equiv.apply_symm_apply,
      ← Precoverage.toGrothendieck_toCoverage, Coverage.mem_toGrothendieck,
      Over.left] at hR
    induction hR with
    | of Z S hS =>
      rw [Sieve.overEquiv_symm_generate]
      exact .of _ _ (by simpa)
    | top =>
      rw [Sieve.overEquiv_symm_top]
      simp
    | transitive Y R S hR H ih ih' =>
      refine GrothendieckTopology.transitive _ (ih s) _ fun Z g hg ↦ ?_
      obtain rfl : right = Z.right := Subsingleton.elim _ _
      rw [← Sieve.overEquiv_symm_pullback]
      exact ih' hg Z.hom
  · rw [Precoverage.toGrothendieck_le_iff_le_toPrecoverage]
    intro Y R hR
    rw [Precoverage.mem_comap_iff] at hR
    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_toPrecoverage_iff, GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff,
      Sieve.overEquiv, Equiv.coe_fn_mk, ← Sieve.generate_map_eq_functorPushforward]
    exact Precoverage.Saturate.of _ _ hR

end

module

public import Mathlib.Topology.Sets.Opens
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Basic

/-!
# Opens and Over categories

In this file, given a topological space `X`, and `U : Opens X`,
we show that the category `Over U` (whose objects are the
`V : Opens X` equipped with a morphism `V ⟶ U`) is equivalent
to the category `Opens U`.

## TODO
* show that both functors of the equivalence `overEquivalence U` are continuous and
  induce an equivalence between `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A`
  and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A` for any category `A`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe u

open CategoryTheory Topology

namespace TopologicalSpace

variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)

namespace Opens

/-- If `X` is a topological space and `U : Opens X`,
then the category `Over U` is equivalent to `Opens ↥U`. -/
@[simps!]
def overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U where
  functor.obj V := ⟨_, IsOpen.preimage (continuous_subtype_val) V.left.isOpen⟩
  functor.map f := homOfLE (Set.preimage_mono (f := Subtype.val) (leOfHom f.left))
  inverse.obj W :=
    Over.mk (Y := ⟨_, (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 W.isOpen⟩)
      (homOfLE (fun _ _ ↦ by aesop))
  inverse.map f := Over.homMk (homOfLE (Set.image_mono (leOfHom f)))
  unitIso := NatIso.ofComponents (fun V ↦ Over.isoMk (eqToIso (by
    ext x
    dsimp
    simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_image, Set.mem_preimage,
      Subtype.exists, exists_and_left, exists_prop, exists_eq_right_right, iff_self_and]
    apply leOfHom V.hom)))
  counitIso := NatIso.ofComponents (fun V ↦ eqToIso (by aesop))

end Opens

end TopologicalSpace

  [∀ X Y, ((J.over X).over Y).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]

#adaptation_note /-- After nightly-2026-02-23 we need this to avoid timeouts. -/
/-- Given an cover `X` and a quasicoherent data for `M` restricted onto each `Mᵢ`, we may glue them
into a quasicoherent data of `M` itself. -/
noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.bind {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) (D : Π i, QuasicoherentData (M.over (X i))) :
    M.QuasicoherentData where
  I := Σ i, (D i).I
  X ij := ((D ij.1).X ij.2).left
  coversTop Y := J.transitive (hX Y) _ fun Z f ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ ↦
      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))
  presentation i :=
    letI e := pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv ((D i.1).X i.2))
      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)
    (((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso
      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over (X i.1)).over ((D i.1).X i.2)))).hom

lemma IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}
    (X : I → C) (hX : J.CoversTop X) [∀ i, IsQuasicoherent (M.over (X i))] :
    IsQuasicoherent M :=
  (QuasicoherentData.bind M X hX fun _ ↦
    IsQuasicoherent.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some).isQuasicoherent

end bind

[e.functor.IsCocontinuous J K] [e.inverse.IsCocontinuous K J] :
    e.inverse.IsDenseSubsite K J :=

variable [e.inverse.IsDenseSubsite K J]

instance : e.functor.IsDenseSubsite J K := by

  infer_instance

/-- The functor in the equivalence of sheaf categories. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafCongr.functor : Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf K A :=
  ObjectProperty.lift _
    (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj e.inverse.op)
    (e.inverse.op_comp_isSheaf _ _)

/-- The inverse in the equivalence of sheaf categories. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafCongr.inverse : Sheaf K A ⥤ Sheaf J A :=
  ObjectProperty.lift _
    (sheafToPresheaf _ _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj e.functor.op)
    (e.functor.op_comp_isSheaf _ _)

/-- The unit iso in the equivalence of sheaf categories. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafCongr.unitIso : 𝟭 (Sheaf J A) ≅ functor J K e A ⋙ inverse J K e A :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun F ↦ ObjectProperty.isoMk _ (isoWhiskerRight e.op.unitIso F.obj))

/-- The counit iso in the equivalence of sheaf categories. -/
@[simps!]
def sheafCongr.counitIso : inverse J K e A ⋙ functor J K e A ≅ 𝟭 (Sheaf _ A) :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun F ↦ ObjectProperty.isoMk _ (isoWhiskerRight e.op.counitIso F.obj))

/-- The equivalence of sheaf categories. -/
@[simps]
def sheafCongr : Sheaf J A ≌ Sheaf K A where
  functor := sheafCongr.functor J K e A
  inverse := sheafCongr.inverse J K e A
  unitIso := sheafCongr.unitIso J K e A
  counitIso := sheafCongr.counitIso J K e A
  functor_unitIso_comp X := by
    ext
    simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]

variable [HasSheafify K A]

/-- Transport a presheaf to the equivalent category and sheafify there. -/
noncomputable
def transportAndSheafify : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ Sheaf J A :=
  e.op.congrLeft.functor ⋙ presheafToSheaf _ _ ⋙ (e.sheafCongr J K A).inverse

/-- An auxiliary definition for the sheafification adjunction. -/
noncomputable
def transportIsoSheafToPresheaf : (e.sheafCongr J K A).functor ⋙
    sheafToPresheaf K A ⋙ e.op.congrLeft.inverse ≅ sheafToPresheaf J A :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun F ↦ isoWhiskerRight e.op.unitIso.symm F.obj)

/-- Transporting and sheafifying is left adjoint to taking the underlying presheaf. -/
noncomputable
def transportSheafificationAdjunction : transportAndSheafify J K e A ⊣ sheafToPresheaf J A :=
  ((e.op.congrLeft.toAdjunction.comp (sheafificationAdjunction _ _)).comp
    (e.sheafCongr J K A).symm.toAdjunction).ofNatIsoRight
    (transportIsoSheafToPresheaf _ _ _ _)

noncomputable instance : PreservesFiniteLimits <| transportAndSheafify J K e A where
  preservesFiniteLimits _ := comp_preservesLimitsOfShape _ _

instance (X : C) : (Over.forget X).IsCocontinuous (J.over X) J where
  cover_lift hS := J.overEquiv_symm_mem_over _ _ hS

instance (X : C) : (Over.forget X).IsContinuous (J.over X) J :=
  Functor.isContinuous_of_coverPreserving
    (over_forget_compatiblePreserving J X)
    (over_forget_coverPreserving J X)

/-- The pullback functor `Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf (J.over X) A` -/
abbrev overPullback (A : Type u') [Category.{v'} A] (X : C) :
    Sheaf J A ⥤ Sheaf (J.over X) A :=
  (Over.forget X).sheafPushforwardContinuous _ _ _

lemma over_map_coverPreserving {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    CoverPreserving (J.over X) (J.over Y) (Over.map f) where
  cover_preserve {U S} hS := by
    obtain ⟨S, rfl⟩ := (Sieve.overEquiv U).symm.surjective S
    rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_over_map]
    apply overEquiv_symm_mem_over
    simpa [mem_over_iff] using hS

    CompatiblePreserving (J.over Y) (Over.map f) where
  compatible {F Z _ x hx Y₁ Y₂ W f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ hg₁ hg₂ h} := by
    let W' : Over X := Over.mk (f₁.left ≫ Y₁.hom)

variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)

namespace Scheme.Opens

/-- Open subset of a scheme as a scheme. -/
@[coe]
def toScheme {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens) : Scheme.{u} :=
  X.restrict U.isOpenEmbedding

instance : CoeOut X.Opens Scheme := ⟨toScheme⟩

/-- The restriction of a scheme to an open subset. -/
def ι : ↑U ⟶ X := X.ofRestrict _

@[simp]
lemma ι_apply (x : U) : U.ι x = x.val := rfl

instance : IsOpenImmersion U.ι := inferInstanceAs (IsOpenImmersion (X.ofRestrict _))

@[simps! over] instance : U.toScheme.CanonicallyOver X where
  hom := U.ι

instance (U : X.Opens) : U.ι.IsOver X where

lemma toScheme_carrier : (U : Type u) = (U : Set X) := rfl

lemma toScheme_presheaf_obj (V) : Γ(U, V) = Γ(X, U.ι ''ᵁ V) := rfl

lemma forall_toScheme {U : X.Opens} {P : U.toScheme → Prop} :
    (∀ x, P x) ↔ ∀ (x : X) (hx : x ∈ U), P ⟨x, hx⟩ := Subtype.forall

lemma exists_toScheme {U : X.Opens} {P : U.toScheme → Prop} :
    (∃ x, P x) ↔ ∃ (x : X) (hx : x ∈ U), P ⟨x, hx⟩ := Subtype.exists

@[simp]
lemma toScheme_presheaf_map {V W} (i : V ⟶ W) :
    U.toScheme.presheaf.map i = X.presheaf.map (U.ι.opensFunctor.map i.unop).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ι_app (V) : U.ι.app V = X.presheaf.map
    (homOfLE (x := U.ι ''ᵁ U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) (Set.image_preimage_subset _ _)).op :=
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma ι_appTop :
    U.ι.appTop = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (x := U.ι ''ᵁ ⊤) le_top).op :=
  rfl

local notation "IsOpenImmersion" => PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion

attribute [instance] IsOpenImmersion.c_iso

section

variable {X Y : PresheafedSpace C} (f : X ⟶ Y) [H : IsOpenImmersion f]

/-- The functor `Opens X ⥤ Opens Y` associated with an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`. -/
abbrev opensFunctor :=
  H.base_open.functor

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- An open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y` induces an isomorphism `X ≅ Y|_{f(X)}`. -/
@[simps! hom_c_app]
noncomputable def isoRestrict : X ≅ Y.restrict H.base_open :=
  PresheafedSpace.isoOfComponents (Iso.refl _) <| by
    symm
    fapply NatIso.ofComponents
    · intro U

def IsOpenMap.functorMap {X Y : TopCat.{u}} {f : X ⟶ Y} {U V : Opens X}
     (HU : IsOpen (f '' U)) (HV : IsOpen (f '' V)) (le : U ≤ V) :
     (⟨_, HU⟩ : Opens Y) ⟶ ⟨_, HV⟩ := ⟨⟨Set.image_mono le⟩⟩

/-- An open map `f : X ⟶ Y` induces a functor `Opens X ⥤ Opens Y`.
-/
@[simps obj_coe]
def IsOpenMap.functor {X Y : TopCat} {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : IsOpenMap f) : Opens X ⥤ Opens Y where
  obj U := ⟨f '' (U : Set X), hf (U : Set X) U.2⟩
  map {U V} h := IsOpenMap.functorMap (hf _ U.2) (hf _ V.2) h.down.down

/-- An open map `f : X ⟶ Y` induces an adjunction between `Opens X` and `Opens Y`.
-/
def IsOpenMap.adjunction {X Y : TopCat} {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
    hf.functor ⊣ Opens.map f where
  unit := { app := fun _ => homOfLE fun x hxU => ⟨x, hxU, rfl⟩ }
  counit := { app := fun _ => homOfLE fun _ ⟨_, hfxV, hxy⟩ => hxy ▸ hfxV }

instance IsOpenMap.functorFullOfMono {X Y : TopCat} {f : X ⟶ Y} (hf : IsOpenMap f) [H : Mono f] :

@[to_dual unitInv_app_inverse]
theorem unit_app_inverse (e : C ≌ D) (Y : D) :
    e.unit.app (e.inverse.obj Y) = e.inverse.map (e.counitInv.app Y) := by
  simpa using Iso.comp_hom_eq_id (e.inverse.mapIso (e.counitIso.app Y)) (f := e.unit.app _)

@[to_dual none, reassoc, simp]
theorem fun_inv_map (e : C ≌ D) (X Y : D) (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    e.functor.map (e.inverse.map f) = e.counit.app X ≫ f ≫ e.counitInv.app Y :=
  (NatIso.naturality_2 e.counitIso f).symm

@[to_dual none, reassoc, simp]
theorem inv_fun_map (e : C ≌ D) (X Y : C) (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    e.inverse.map (e.functor.map f) = e.unitInv.app X ≫ f ≫ e.unit.app Y :=
  (NatIso.naturality_1 e.unitIso f).symm

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)

This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.

The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
  the open immersion `U.ι`;
* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of the
  sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the geometric
  ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).

From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).

Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/

section OverRestrictBridge

open TopologicalSpace Topology

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)

/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).

For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules` of
sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and the
identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).

Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
    (by ext : 2
        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app, Category.id_comp,
          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
    (by ext : 2
        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
          Functor.comp_obj]
        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
        rename_i x
        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
            = 𝟙 _ := by
          rw [← op_comp]
          simp
        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm

end Scheme.Modules

end OverRestrictBridge

end AlgebraicGeometry

lemma pushforward_comp_id :
    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl

lemma pushforward_id_comp :
    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl

end

section NatTrans

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D}
  {F G H : C ⥤ D} {T : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {S : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  [Functor.IsContinuous G J K]
  [Functor.IsContinuous H J K]
  (φ : T ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S)

/-- A natural transformation gives a natural transformation between the pushforward functors. -/
noncomputable
def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
    pushforward.{v} φ ⟶
      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α _ _ _).app S) where
  app X :=
  { val.app U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.hom.app U).hom).map (X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op)
    val.naturality {U V} i := by
      ext x
      dsimp
      change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
        (X.val.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
      simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
  naturality {X Y} f := by
    ext U x
    exact congr($(f.val.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm

@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app (α : F ⟶ G) (M U x) :
    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).val.app U x = M.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforwardNatTrans_id :
    pushforwardNatTrans φ (𝟙 G) = (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch

@[simp]
lemma pushforwardNatTrans_comp (α : F ⟶ G) (β : G ⟶ H)
    (φ : T ⟶ (H.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S) :
    pushforwardNatTrans φ (α ≫ β) = pushforwardNatTrans φ β ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ α ≫
      (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch

@[simp]
lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (X U x) :
    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app X).val.app U x = X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl

/-- A natural isomorphism gives a natural isomorphism between the pushforward functors. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def pushforwardNatIso (α : F ≅ G) :
    pushforward.{v} φ ≅
      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α.hom _ _ _).app S) where
  hom := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.hom
  inv := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.inv ≫
    (pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp])).hom
  hom_inv_id := by
    ext X U x
    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op ≫
      X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
      Iso.inv_hom_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
  inv_hom_id := by
    ext X U x
    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op ≫

/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the open immersion's
opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the two
ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; rfl)

/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a presentation
of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local. -/

    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M

end Scheme.Modules

- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean):**
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictEquiv`,

- **sorry count in file: 4 → 4** (unchanged). The 4 are the pre-existing protected stubs
  `hilbertPolynomial` @123, `QuotFunctor` @161, `Grassmannian` @198, `Grassmannian.representable` @225.

- **Axioms:** `overRestrictEquiv`, `overRestrictFunctorIso`, `overRestrictIso`,
  `overRestrictPullbackIso` all `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
- **`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`: SUCCESS** (8317 jobs). Only warnings are the 4
  pre-existing stub sorries and 4 harmless `Sheaf.Hom.mk` deprecation notices (alias of
  `ObjectProperty.homMk`; kept because the same token appears in the proofs' `simp only` sets).

## The objective was COMPLETED

PROGRESS.md lane 2 asked: "build step 2 (geometric ring-sheaf identification) → step 3 → step 4
⟹ `overRestrictIso`, as far as axiom-clean progress allows. … Naming the new decl
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictIso` (the blueprint `\lean{}` pin) is the target."
**All four steps landed; the pinned target `overRestrictIso` exists and is axiom-clean.**

## overRestrictEquiv (def, ~line 929) — steps 2 + 3 — RESOLVED

- **Approach:** The "current obstacle" step 2 (identify `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U` with
  `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` transported across `overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat`) turned out to
  hold **by `rfl`** at the pinned commit. Two definitional facts unlock it:
  - `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` is `rfl`
    (verified standalone).
  - hence `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
    (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` is `rfl` (verified standalone). This is exactly the blueprint Step-2
    ring-sheaf identification — the geometric obstacle dissolves definitionally because
    `U.toScheme.presheaf = U.ι.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf` is `rfl` (Mathlib `toScheme_presheaf_obj`
    / `toScheme_presheaf_map`).
  - **Step 3** then is `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Opens.overEquivalence U)
    φ ψ H₁ H₂`, with `φ := whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (R.over U).obj` (the unit of the
    site equivalence whiskered into the structure presheaf) and `ψ := 𝟙` (legal because the geometric
    ring sheaf is *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one). The two coherence fields:
    - `H₁`: `ext : 2` then `congrArg …obj.map (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op
      (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm)` — the standard equivalence identity
      `e.unitInv.app (e.inverse.obj Y) = e.inverse.map (e.counit.app Y)`.
    - `H₂`: `ext : 2`; `simp only [...]`; `erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]`; then
      `(unitIso.inv.app x).op ≫ (unit.app x).op = 𝟙` via `rw [← op_comp]; simp` and close by
      `congrArg map h |>.trans (Functor.map_id ..)`.
  - Took `.symm` to orient as `SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.
- **Dead-end note:** `rw [Category.id_comp]` / `rw [← op_comp]` fail by *syntactic* pattern-match even
  when the subterm is visibly present (the `Over`/opposite-category `Category` instance is reducibly
  but not syntactically the expected one). Use `erw` for `id_comp`, and discharge the op-composition
  identity in a separate `have` then `exact (congrArg map h).trans (Functor.map_id ..)` (defeq-aware).
  Do NOT retry `rw [h]`/`rw [Category.id_comp]` here.

## overRestrictFunctorIso (def, ~line 963) + overRestrictIso (def, ~line 978) — step 4 — RESOLVED

- **Approach:** Functor-level identification `(over · U) ⋙ overRestrictEquiv.functor ≅
  restrictFunctor U.ι`. Both sides are `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along the *same* opens functor
  (`eqv.inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`), so:
  `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)`.
  - Needed an explicit `haveI : (eqv.inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous _ _ :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp …` (composition continuity is not auto-synthesised).
  - The ring-morphism equality fed to `pushforwardCongr` (`𝟙 ≫ map 𝟙` vs `restrictFunctor`'s
    `whiskerRight α (forget₂)` with `α = (U.ι.appIso _).inv = 𝟙` since `ι_appIso = Iso.refl`) is closed
    by **`cat_disch`** (manual `ext;simp` stalls on the `Sheaf.Hom`/`.hom.app` wrapper — `cat_disch`
    grinds it).
  - `overRestrictIso M := (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. This is the blueprint-pinned target.
- **Blueprint NOTE satisfied:** the chapter `% NOTE` warned the literal form "must be routed THROUGH
  the step-3 equivalence functor (the two sides live in different module categories)". `overRestrictIso`
  is stated exactly as `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`
  — routed through the equivalence functor, as anticipated. No signature sharpening of the chapter
  is needed beyond confirming this object-level shape.

## overRestrictPullbackIso (def, ~line 989) — step 4' bonus — RESOLVED

- **Approach:** `overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M`, landing
  `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M`. This is the
  pullback form the blueprint Step-4 prose names ("composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands
  the geometric restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`"), and the form the downstream `Presentation`
  transport (P1) consumes.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Needs blueprint entry

All four are non-private `def`s in `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` with **no blueprint block yet**
(`lean_aux` nodes — `archon dag-query unmatched` will list 3 of them; `overRestrictIso` itself already
has `lem:over_restrict_iso`). Restore the 1-to-1 Lean↔blueprint correspondence next iter:

  **Relies on:** `def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr`, the two `overEquivalence_*_isContinuous` instances,

  **Relies on:** `overRestrictEquiv`, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp`, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`,

- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`** — NEW, no block. Step 4' (pullback
  form). **Relies on:** `overRestrictIso`, Mathlib `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`.

## Why I stopped
